$comment: Mechanically-enforced gate baselines (see EVALS.md rules). run-routing.mjs prints a gate verdict on every run and exits 1 on breach when invoked with --gate (CI does). Re-baselining is an explicit act: update these numbers in the same commit as the change that moves them, with the decision recorded in Solo (EVALS.md rule 1). Counts are absolute hits, not percentages.
note: re-baselined 2026-07-13 on the skills-form arm-B ship (Solo scratchpad 608 P4; todo 890): the 204 skill-section entries carry searchable:false and leave search while whole-skill entries, section reads (skill.read + availableSections), and the runnable digest are unchanged. Decided by a pre-registered, adversarially pre-reviewed A/B: offline legacy strict 205/272/305 -> 209/285/316 with skills lane 18/23 at floor, extended and overlay improved, interloper capture 68->56 top-1 / 278->214 top-5; paid paired blocks (3x QA-30 per arm, counterbalanced) scored A 39C/42P/9W vs B 41C/42P/7W over 90 gradings with stable per-case wins 3-1 for B and a flat skills stratum (OpenZeppelin correct 6/6 via whole-skill discovery); guards: carried live-v2 ten (B zero wrongs across 3 runs) and 3x agent discovery (B >= A on every overall metric every run). The skills floor stays minTop1 18 (B hits exactly 18: q-skill-eco-scout-rwa-landscape moved off top-1 to top-3, the whole-skill entry still ranks). Prior baseline: routing-2026-07-12T21-12-46-662Z.json (Scout 1.7.16 drift + lever 7, issue #21).
